skill.name: libreoffice-impress
skill.description: Use when creating, editing, formatting, or extracting LibreOffice Impress (.odp) presentations via UNO, including session-based slide edits, structured targets, lists, tables, charts, media, notes, master pages, patch workflows, and snapshots.
skill.summary: Automate LibreOffice Impress presentations with session-based editing, patch workflows, and PNG export.
skill.author: dfk1352

security_audit.summary: This skill provides LibreOffice Impress presentation automation via UNO API. Static analysis flagged 210 potential issues, but manual review confirms all are false positives or legitimate office automation patterns. Subprocess calls invoke hardcoded LibreOffice commands for document processing. No network exfiltration, credential access, or persistence mechanisms detected. Risk level is LOW - appropriate for publication.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Subprocess Usage for LibreOffice
low_findings.description: The skill uses subprocess to invoke LibreOffice soffice commands. These are hardcoded commands with no user input injection. This is standard office automation behavior required for UNO API integration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run and subprocess.Popen use hardcoded soffice commands with controlled arguments. No user input flows into command arguments.

low_findings.title: Safe Temporary File Handling
low_findings.description: Uses Python tempfile module for temporary files during presentation processing. This is standard practice for office automation with proper cleanup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Uses tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() and tempfile.mkdtemp() with proper cleanup via shutil.rmtree(). No security risk.

content.user_title: Create and edit LibreOffice Impress presentations programmatically
content.value_statement: Generate, modify, and export professional presentations with automated slide operations. This skill provides programmatic control over LibreOffice Impress for bulk presentation workflows.
actual_capabilities: Create new Impress presentations from scratch with custom layouts
Edit existing presentations: add, delete, move, duplicate slides
Insert and format text, shapes, images, tables, charts, and media
Apply patch workflows using structured DSL syntax
Export presentations to PDF or PPTX formats
Capture slide snapshots as PNG images for preview
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installation on the host system
Cannot process password-protected or encrypted presentations
Chart and media insertion may require additional LibreOffice components

use_cases.title: Automated Report Generation
use_cases.description: Generate weekly or monthly reports as presentations by merging data into slide templates. Add charts, tables, and formatted text programmatically.

use_cases.title: Bulk Presentation Updates
use_cases.description: Apply consistent changes across multiple presentations. Update company branding, fix text across decks, or add standard footers to all slides.

use_cases.title: Slide Content Extraction
use_cases.description: Extract text content, speaker notes, or slide inventory from existing presentations for documentation or analysis purposes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Impress presentation at /path/to/output.odp with a title slide and two content slides.

prompt_templates.title: Edit existing presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Open /path/to/report.odp and replace all instances of 'Q3 2024' with 'Q4 2024' in the body text.

prompt_templates.title: Apply patch operations
prompt_templates.prompt:
Apply this patch to /path/to/deck.odp:
[operation]
type = insert_text_box
target.kind = slide
target.slide_index = 2
...
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a specific patch DSL to apply

prompt_templates.title: Export and capture slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Export /path/to/presentation.odp to PDF at /path/to/output.pdf, then capture a PNG snapshot of slide 3.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need both export and preview

output_examples.input: Create a presentation with title 'Sales Report'
output_examples.output: Successfully created presentation with 1 slide using BLANK layout. The title placeholder is ready for content.
Patch applied successfully (atomic mode). Operations: replace_text [ok], insert_text_box [ok]. Document persisted.

best_practices: Use absolute file paths for all presentation files to avoid path resolution issues
Prefer shape names over shape indices for more stable target references
Use atomic patch mode for production workflows to prevent partial document corruption
anti_patterns: Do not use relative paths when calling Impress APIs
Avoid relying on exact shape names after LibreOffice-native slide duplication
Do not skip slide inventory after mutations that change deck order

faq.question: Does this skill require LibreOffice to be installed?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses LibreOffice UNO API for all presentation operations. LibreOffice must be installed and accessible via the soffice command.

faq.question: Can I edit password-protected presentations?
faq.answer: No. The UNO API does not support editing encrypted or password-protected presentations. You must remove protection first.

faq.question: What slide index system does Impress use?
faq.answer: Impress uses zero-based indexing. The first slide has index 0, the second has index 1, and so on.

faq.question: How do I target a specific text element?
faq.answer: Use ImpressTarget with kind=text, slide_index, and optionally placeholder (title, body, subtitle) or text to narrow matches.

faq.question: What is the difference between atomic and best_effort patch modes?
faq.answer: Atomic mode stops on first failure and restores the original file. Best_effort mode applies successful operations and records failures.

faq.question: Can I export presentations to formats other than PDF?
faq.answer: Yes. Supported export formats are PDF and PPTX. PPTX export uses LibreOffice's PowerPoint 2007 XML filter.
